# Matter / Thread partition table — ESP3: # Laid out for OTA from the start so enabling OTA later never needs a repartition
# (a repartition = full erase = the Matter fabric is wiped and the device must be
# re-commissioned). The OTA *update flow* is future work; the slots exist now.
# Layout rationale:
#   - App (ota_0/ota_1) partitions must be 64 KB (0x10000) aligned. The table packs the
#     data partitions into the first 0x20000 and splits the rest of the 4 MB evenly into
#     two 0x1F0000 (1.9375 MB) app slots — no gaps
#   - A size-optimized C6 Matter sensor (MINIMAL_BUILD) is ~1.79 MB
4 MB flash: so it fits a slot with

# Matter / Thread partition table — ESP3: #     ~150 KB headroom (~92% full). If feature growth ever exceeds a slot
#     OTA (esp_delta_ota is already a dependency) rather than shrinking the second slot.
#   - nvs   = 48 KB: Matter + Thread operational/config storage.
#   - fctry = 32 KB: reserved for factory data / Device Attestation Cert (DAC). Unused while
#             running on esp-matter built-in TEST credentials (fine for Home Assistant);
#             populated by mfg_tool only if per-device certs are needed later.
# Partition table offset is 0x8000 (CONFIG_PARTITION_TABLE_OFFSET).
# Name
nvs
otadata
phy_init
fctry
ota_0
ota_1
